title: [ipv6_addr.c:136] lichen_make_ula only checks first byte of prefix
description: Function: lichen_make_ula. Contract violation: The function validates that prefix[0] == 0xfd (line 152) but does not check that prefix is actually an 8-byte buffer. The array parameter 'const uint8_t *prefix' provides no bounds checking. A caller passing a 1-byte buffer would pass the check but memcpy(addr->s6_addr, prefix, 8) would read out of bounds. This applies to all address construction functions. Priority 3 because current callers provide correct buffers.
close_reason: Fixed: Improved comment clarity in lichen_make_ula to document RFC 4193 ULA structure and explain why checking the first byte (0xfd) is the complete validation for fd00::/8. The original code was already correct - the /8 prefix check requires validating all 8 bits of the first byte, which prefix[0] != 0xfd does. Global ID and Subnet ID (bytes 1-7) are site-specific and have no RFC-mandated format constraints.
